memoryArtifacts.headline: Shelton Family Tree
memoryArtifacts.description:
https://sheltonfamilyhistory.wordpress.com/tag/shelton-family-tree/

John Shelton, 1st Lord of Shelton, was born about 1140 in Shelton, Norfolk, England.  He was Lord Mayor in Stradbrooke, Suffolk, En …

Origin Displayed: English - Spelling variations of this family name include: Shelton, Sheltone and others. First found in Yorkshire where they were seated from early times and their first records appeared on the census rolls taken by the ancient Kings of Britain to determine the rate of taxation of their subjects. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: Richard and Francis Shelton who settled in Virginia in 1638; Samuel and Esther Shelton settled in Boston Mass. in 1765 with Hannah; Samuel Shelton settled in the Barbados with his servants in 1680. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). This interesting surname, with variant spellings Shilton and Skelton is found widespread in the county of Nottinghamshire. The name itself is of English habitational origin from any of the places so called in Nottinghamshire, Bedfordshire, Norfolk, Shropshire and Staffordshire. The first of these was recorded "Sceltun", in the Domesday Book of 1086, while this source also records the other place names "Eseltone", "Sceltuna", "Saltone", and "Scelfitone", respectively. The place name is composed of the elements "scylf", the Old English word for shelf, ledge, or bank and "tun", the Old English word describing a settlement or enclosure. One John de Schelton appeared in 1260 in the Assize Court Rolls of Yorkshire, while the Assize Court Rolls of Warwickshire record a John Shelton in 1385. Notable name bearers include one Thomas Shelton (died 1620) who was the first translator of "Don Quixote" into English and one Sir Richard Shelton (died 1647) employed by the Duke of Buckingham and appointed solicitor-general (1625-1634) becoming M.P. for Bridgnorth (1626-1628) and treasurer of the Inner Temple in 1628. The first recorded spelling of the family name is shown to be that of Robert de Schelton, which was dated 1191, in the Pipe Rolls of Staffordshire, during the reign of King Richard 1st, known as "The Lionheart", 1189 - 1199. Surnames became necessary when governments introduced personal taxation. In England this was known as Poll Tax. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling.  Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 – 2014.

zh.Shelton,Norfolk,England: 英國英格蘭諾福爾可雪登
zh.St. Mary's Church, Eryholme, Yorkshire,: 英國英格蘭越可爾艾利格爾梅聖 馬也斯 邱奇
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John De Shelton, III於1249年出生在英國諾福爾可雪登時，他的父親Sir Ralph de Shelton, 6th Lord of Shelton Manor21歲，母親Ms. Ralph De Shelton18歲。他於大約1281年與Alice de Burgullion在英國英格蘭諾福爾可雪登結婚。他們至少是10個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1333年在他的家鄉享年84歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾艾利格爾梅聖 馬也斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.Shelton,Norfolk,England: 英国英格兰諾福爾可雪登
zh-hans.St. Mary's Church, Eryholme, Yorkshire,: 英国英格兰越可爾艾利格爾梅聖 馬也斯 邱奇
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John De Shelton, III于1249年出生在英国諾福爾可雪登时，他的父亲Sir Ralph de Shelton, 6th Lord of Shelton Manor21岁，母亲Ms. Ralph De Shelton18岁。他于大约1281年与Alice de Burgullion在英国英格兰諾福爾可雪登结婚。他们至少是10个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1333年在他的家乡享年84岁，埋葬在英国英格兰越可爾艾利格爾梅聖 馬也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Shelton,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire): habitational name primarily from Shelton (Nottinghamshire), but also from Shelton (Bedfordshire, Norfolk, Shropshire, and Staffordshire). The placenames all derive from Old English scelf ‘rock, ledge, shelf’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

nameMeanings.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John De Shelton, III was born in 1249, in Shelton, Norfolk, England, his father, Sir Ralph de Shelton, 6th Lord of Shelton Manor, was 21 and his mother, Ms. Ralph De Shelton, was 18. He married Alice de Burgullion about 1281, in Shelton, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 10 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1333, in his hometown, at the age of 84, and was buried in St. Mary's Church, Eryholme, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John De Shelton, III est né en 1249, à Shelton, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Sir Ralph de Shelton, 6th Lord of Shelton Manor, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Ms. Ralph De Shelton, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Alice de Burgullion environ 1281, à Shelton, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 10 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1333, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 84 ans, et a été enterré à St. Mary's Church, Eryholme, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John De Shelton, III 1249 in Shelton, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Ralph de Shelton, 6th Lord of Shelton Manor 21 und seine Mutter Ms. Ralph De Shelton 18. Er hat um 1281 in Shelton, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom Alice de Burgullion geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 10 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1333 im Alter von 84 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in St. Mary's Church, Eryholme, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John De Shelton, III è nato il nel 1249 nel Shelton, Norfolk, England, il suo padre, Sir Ralph de Shelton, 6th Lord of Shelton Manor, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Ms. Ralph De Shelton, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Alice de Burgullion circa 1281, nel Shelton, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 10 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1333, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 84 anni, e fu sepolto a St. Mary's Church, Eryholme, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John De Shelton, IIIが1249年にイギリスノーフォルクシェルトンで生まれた時，父Sir Ralph de Shelton, 6th Lord of Shelton Manorは21歳，母Ms. Ralph De Sheltonは18歳であった。約1281年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクシェルトンでAlice de Burgullionと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも10人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1333年に故郷で84歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアエリhオルメスト. マリ'ス チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.St. Mary's Church, Eryholme, Yorkshire,: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 리홀 스트. 마이'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Sir John De Shelton, III(이)가 1249년 영국 노르폴크 셸턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Ralph de Shelton, 6th Lord of Shelton Manor(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Ms. Ralph De Shelton(은)는 18세였다. 그는 대략 1281년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 셸턴에서 Alice de Burgullion(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1333년 그의 고향에서 84세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 리홀 스트. 마이'스 c후르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John De Shelton, III nasceu em 1249, em Shelton, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Ralph de Shelton, 6th Lord of Shelton Manor, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Ms. Ralph De Shelton, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice de Burgullion aproximadamente 1281, em Shelton, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 10 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1333, em sua cidade natal, com 84 anos, e foi sepultado em St. Mary's Church, Eryholme, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.St. Mary's Church, Eryholme, Yorkshire,: ст. мэри'с чурч, эрихолм, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John De Shelton, III родился в 1249 г. в местности шелтон, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Ralph de Shelton, 6th Lord of Shelton Manor было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Ms. Ralph De Shelton, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice de Burgullion около 1281 г. в местности шелтон, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 10 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1333 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 84 лет и был похоронен в местности ст. мэри'с чурч, эрихолм, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Alice de Burgullion aproximadamente 1281, en Shelton, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 10 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1333, en su pueblo, a la edad de 84 años, y fue sepultado en St. Mary's Church, Eryholme, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
